content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

November 17, 1989


Frances Hong, City Clerk
City of Stockton
Stockton, CA 95202

Re: Combined Campaign Report
Our File No. I-89-619

Dear Ms. Hong:

This is to confirm advice provided to your office on October
20, 1989, regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ This letter constitutes written advice under Section 84205.

Section 84205 states:

The Commission may by regulation or written advice
permit candidates and committees to file campaign
statements combining statements and reports
required to be filed by this title.

A special election has been called to amend and revise the
City of Stockton's charter. The election date is February 6,
1990. Because the second pre-election statement due in connection
with the special election (Section 84200.8) and the semi-annual
statement due for the last six months of 1989 (Section 84200)
cover overlapping periods, we believe it is appropriate for
committees primarily formed to support or oppose the ballot
measure in that election to combine those two reports.

Therefore, campaign disclosure statements should be filed on
the following schedule:


1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Frances Hong
*File No. I-89-619
Page 1


Type of Statement                Period Covered              Filing Deadline

1st Pre-Election                 Jan. 1² - Dec. 23          December 28, 1989

2nd Pre-Election                 Dec. 24 - Jan. 20          January 25, 1990
Combined with semi-annual
statement for the second
half of 1989

Semi-Annual                      Jan. 21 - June 30          July 31, 1990


        If you have any questions regarding the above, please do not
hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5662.

                                                Sincerely,

                                                Kathryn E. Donovan
                                                General Counsel

                                                [signature]

                                                By: Lynda Cassady
                                                    Political Reform Consultant

KED:LC:eab




2       The period covered by any statement begins on the day after the
closing date of the last statement filed or January 1 if no
previous statement has been filed.

sections.conclusion: Because the second pre-election statement due in connection with the special election (Section 84200.8) and the semi-annual statement due for the last six months of 1989 (Section 84200) cover overlapping periods, we believe it is appropriate for committees primarily formed to support or oppose the ballot measure in that election to combine those two reports.
sections.question_synthetic: May candidates and committees in Stockton's February 6, 1990 special election to amend the charter combine the second pre-election statement and the semi-annual statement for the last six months of 1989, given that these reporting periods overlap?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC permits committees primarily formed to support or oppose the ballot measure to combine the second pre-election statement and the semi-annual statement for the last six months of 1989 due to overlapping coverage periods. The FPPC provides a specific filing schedule with three reporting periods and corresponding deadlines.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document follows standard FPPC advice letter format. No explicit 'QUESTION' or 'CONCLUSION' section headers, but the question and conclusion are clearly stated in the body text. This is written advice under Government Code section 84205. File number I-89-619.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: May candidates and committees in Stockton's February 6, 1990 special election to amend the charter combine the second pre-election statement and the semi-annual statement for the last six months of 1989, given that these reporting periods overlap?

CONCLUSION: Because the second pre-election statement due in connection with the special election (Section 84200.8) and the semi-annual statement due for the last six months of 1989 (Section 84200) cover overlapping periods, we believe it is appropriate for committees primarily formed to support or oppose the ballot measure in that election to combine those two reports.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advises the City of Stockton that campaign committees may combine the second pre-election statement and semi-annual statement for overlapping reporting periods related to the February 6, 1990 special election charter amendment vote, and establishes a filing schedule with deadlines of December 28, 1989, January 25, 1990, and July 31, 1990.
